name: Calzone
recipeInstructions:
For calzones, unroll the pizza dough. Roll or stretch the dough into a 15x10-inch rectangle. Cut into 6 5-inch squares. Divide the desired filling among the squares.
Brush the edges with water. Lift one corner and stretch the dough over to the opposite corner. Press the edges of the dough well with a fork to seal.
Arrange on a greased baking sheet. Prick the tops with a fork. Combine egg and 1 tablespoon water. Brush onto calzones.
Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese, if desired. Bake at 425°F for 8-10 minutes. Let stand 5 minutes before serving. Makes 6 calzones.
Sausage-Mushroom Filling: In a skillet, cook 12 ounces bulk pork sausage or Italian sausage until brown. Drain. Stir in 1/2 cup pizza sauce, one 4 ounce package shredded mozzarella cheese (1 cup), and 1 8-ounce can mushroom stems and pieces, drained.
Ham-Spinach Filling: Cook 1 10 ounce package frozen chopped spinach according to package directions; drain well. In a bowl combine spinach, 1 cup finely chopped fully cooked ham (5 ounces), 1 cup shredded Swiss cheese (4 ounces), and 2 tablespoons thinly sliced green onion.
